programme.title: Wolf Creek
programme.desc: Discovering that Mick has left them in the middle of nowhere, the group realise that their trip of a lifetime has turned into a desperate bid for survival.

programme.title: Wolf Creek

programme.desc: Residents of California's San Fernando Valley are under attack by flying saucers from outer space. The aliens, led by Eros (Dudley Manlove) and his assistant, Tanna (Joanna Lee), intend to conquer the planet by resurrecting corpses in a Hollywood cemetery. The living dead -- a cape-wearing ghoul (Bela Lugosi), a vampire (Vampira) and a slow-footed cop (Tor Johnson) who was killed for his nosiness -- stalk curious humans who wander into the cemetery looking for evidence of the UFOs.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie helps Sadie with her homework by enlisting the guys to create a real-life version of a math problem, but when Jase and Willie get sidetracked helping Sadie.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Jep asks Willie and the guys to help move his old chicken coop into his new house, which is under renovation, Jessica takes issue with its appearance, leading the guys to yet another project.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Willie becomes an assistant coach on Lil' Will's struggling football team, his authority is tested when the kids challenge the adults to a scrimmage. Meanwhile, Kay brings Jep and Si to an aquarobics class at a local health club.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Jase and the guys transform an old pontoon boat into a floating duck blind, they are faced with the impending question of whether or not it will actually float. Meanwhile, when Kay's dog Bobo misbehaves, Willie signs Kay and Bobo up for a doggie obedience class.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Jep and Jessica move into Willie and Jase's neighborhood, Jep is sidelined with an illness, causing Willie and the Robertson family to pitch in and help Jessica get her new house ready in time for Jep's arrival home.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The Robertson family throws a party for Willie & Korie's 25th anniversary, but things take a dramatic turn when Willie and Korie reveal a big secret. Meanwhile, when Jase blames Uncle Si for getting him a parking ticket.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Willie asks a local chainsaw artist to make him a statue, he gets a big surprise when it's revealed that the statue is of an unlikely subject -- Willie himself. Meanwhile, Jep and Jessica attempt to help Kay with her hoarding.

programme.desc: On a trip to New York, Jase's son Reed proposes to his girlfriend, which inspires his father to fan the flames of romance back home with Missy.

programme.desc: The tables are turned when the HOA comes to Jase for help to rid the neighborhood of a menacing beaver.

programme.desc: When Jase and Jep get into an argument over which of them is the rightful heir to their grandfather's old rifle, they decide to settle the argument with a marksmanship contest.

programme.desc: After Si has started moonlighting as a grocery bagger, the guys show up at the store and their teasing inadvertently causes Si to lose his job and his chance to enter a local bagging competition.

programme.desc: The Robertson family throws a party for Willie & Korie's 25th anniversary, but things take a dramatic turn when Willie and Korie reveal a big secret. Meanwhile, when Jase blames Uncle Si for getting him a parking ticket.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Willie asks a local chainsaw artist to make him a statue, he gets a big surprise when it's revealed that the statue is of an unlikely subject -- Willie himself. Meanwhile, Jep and Jessica attempt to help Kay with her hoarding.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: On a trip to New York, Jase's son Reed proposes to his girlfriend, which inspires his father to fan the flames of romance back home with Missy.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty

programme.title: Duck Dynasty

programme.title: Duck Dynasty

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As April gets under way, Monty Don begins the process of reinvigorating his Jewel Garden where, over the coming months, he will be using plants which will give an evolving tapestry of colour over the seasons. Elsewhere, Carol Klein revels in the glory of one the world's most renowned camellia collections when she visits Mount Edgcumbe on the banks of the River Tamar in Cornwall.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: It's the busiest gardening weekend of the year and Monty has plenty of ideas and inspiration from planting summer bulbs to sowing perennials. New projects are on his mind as he begins to mark out a brand-new area at Longmeadow. Carol Klein profiles the bravest and most colourful of spring flowering plants when she delves into the botany of the hellebore.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The first big gardening weekend of the year gets underway with Monty dividing perennials and giving plenty of tips for how we can kick-start the garden for the season ahead. As millions of bedding plants are poised for planting in our pots and borders, Joe Swift visits a major grower to find out how they are produced and what drives the demand.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reveals plans for a new raised seating area, surrounded by wild flowers. Carol looks at the blossom at Battsford Arboretum in the Cotswolds and now the risk of frosts should have passed, Monty plants beans and courgettes in the vegetable garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don tackles seasonal jobs at Longmeadow and plants clematis in his cottage garden. Rachel de Thame takes a walk down memory lane when she visits the world's biggest flower garden in Holland and Carol Klein is on the trail of early plant hunters, responsible for the glorious selection of magnolias brought to these shores.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don looks ahead to the long Easter weekend by cutting back his grass borders. Meanwhile, Joe Swift reveals his top tips for creating a natural garden, and Carol Klein travels to Cornwall in search of a garden full of spring colour. Plus, the `living catalogue' of new and existing varieties of bedding plants at Ball Colegrave in Banbury, Oxfordshire.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As summer unfolds at Longmeadow, Monty Don undertakes timely tasks in his vegetable garden and flower beds. Meanwhile, Carol Klein enjoys the beauty of one of our unsung native flowers, the geum, and visits a cottage garden which holds the national collection of cultivated forms. Plus, Formula 1 supremo Sir Frank Williams explains why he is opening his garden at Inholmes.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don demonstrates how to prepare for spring and summer with timely tasks in his damp garden and around the pond, and Joe Swift continues his series of master classes on planting design by sharing tips on how to create a tropical green oasis. Elsewhere, Carol Klein plants a viburnum that will give spring colour and fill her Devon garden with fragrance.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With bees in mind, Monty Don plans for spring by planting some early nectar sources for them, using bulbs which flower early in the year, namely crocus and winter aconites. He also selects some asters which will not only give colour for autumn but will also help bees build up their nectar reserves to help them survive the winter. Plus, Carol Klein is in Norfolk at Pensthorpe Nature Reserve.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the winter draws to a close, work in the garden starts to step up gear. With the flowers and fruits of summer in mind, Monty Don gets going by planting pots of summer bulbs for colour and scent, passing on his secrets of success by using a variety of planting techniques. He also reveals plans for reinvigorating the area in his garden where perennials are planted. Plus, Carol Klein visits Glendurgan Garden in Cornwall.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty gets to work in his wildlife garden to ensure that there are plenty of plants in flower for early pollinators. Carol Klein meets Stuart Donachie who has embraced every planting opportunity in his shady garden in Herefordshire, and Frances Tophill invites viewers to grow easy summer vegetables along with her as she trials varieties for taste and productivity at RHS Rosemoor.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don is planning ahead and planting autumn-flowering crocus at Longmeadow. Also for autumn colour, he's adding cyclamen to the spring garden, and has tips on how to get a great show from them. Meanwhile, Joe Swift visits Marks Hall - a surprising but inspiring modern garden and Carol Klein is in Staffordshire with a couple who've created a garden for all seasons in their half-acre plot.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don is at Longmeadow sharing a wealth of tips and ideas on how to keep your garden looking good all year long. He tackles some urgent pruning, and reveals some of the projects he's got in store over the coming months.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At the height of summer it's time to ensure that fruits and vegetables are given attention to ensure maximum cropping. Monty gives advice on the summer pruning of fruit trees as well as showing how to get the best from tomatoes and chillies. He also recommends plants which will carry on flowering into Autumn.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is summer-pruning his trained fruit, sowing some hardy annuals and is planning ahead for winter in the veg garden. Meanwhile, Jamie Butterworth visits RHS Garden Bridgewater in Salford.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is cutting back in the Cottage Garden to make room for annuals, harvesting tomatoes and taking sage cuttings in the Herb Garden. Joe Swift visits a historic allotment site in Nottingham which has been brought back to life.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With a bank holiday weekend to look forward to, Adam Frost has a few things to be getting on with in the garden. His mini-meadow needs some attention, he's got an autumn container to plant up, and in the borders, there are seeds to collect.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty takes salvia cuttings in the Jewel Garden. Adam Frost visits a ceramicist in Kent. Joe Swift heads to an organic flower farm set up as a social enterprise. Plus, a coastal haven planted by a mum to recreate the wild upbringing.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is harvesting sweetcorn. Toby visits a magical garden in Wiltshire. Frances is in a Cornish village to find out how pioneering technology is creating beautiful borders for pollinators. A self-taught grower is transforming her allotment.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don is asked to rescue two nightmare outside spaces. In Salisbury, newly engaged Glenn and Zoe want a brand new garden and most of all they are desperate to produce flowers for their wedding. Currently their steeply sloping garden is completely unusable.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don visits two sets of ambitious gardeners, each facing challenges on a very different scale. One is a tiny urban courtyard in Hackney and the other is a outdoor area for a community.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty travels to two corners of the country where the challenges he faces are very different. On the south coast he is presented with a front garden ravaged by salty winds, and in Snowdonia he helps a mountainside garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty has tips and ideas for planting up garden pots to provide a focal point over the winter months, Rachel visits the tulips of Keukenhof, Joe visits Esther Rantzen's much-loved garden, and Carol visits Glebe Cottage.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: This time there's plenty to do to prepare for the season to come and Monty Don is clearing out spent crops and mulching his asparagus. Joe Swift's been to visit keen gardeners, Wayne and Geraldine Hemingway, to see how they combine sustainability with their designer-style. Carol Klein's exploring the history of the rose at Coughton Court, one of our best rose gardens and Monty plants a new rose of his own. What happens when you have to downsize and leave your much-loved garden?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don prepares the flowering borders for winter and makes a bonfire, Carol celebrates our berry-laden hedgerows, and Rachel visits Julian Clary in his garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Carol is looking at one of the gems of early spring; the crocus, Joe visits a bog garden in west Wales, and Rachel visits a fabulous rose garden with over 2500 different varieties.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Carol looks at the spring delight that is the cyclamen, Joe visits a gardener taking on the challenges of gardening on the windswept Cornish coastline, and Monty tackles the problem of box blight.

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don returns to Japan during the fiery blaze of autumn. He visits the three great gardens of Japan to learn how they protect the garden from the coming winter and learns how the Japanese are weaving nature into their concrete urban jungle.

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don returns to Japan during the fiery blaze of autumn. He visits the three great gardens of Japan to learn how they protect the garden from the coming winter and learns how the Japanese are weaving nature into their concrete urban jungle.

programme.title: Monty Don's Italian Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don conducts a personal grand tour around Italy's magnificent gardens from the luxurious retreats of the moneyed north to the lavish hideaways of the romantic south. In this episode, he's in and around Rome, a powerbase of emperors and popes, to explore gardens that are among some of the most extravagant and flamboyant ever created in history.

programme.title: Monty Don's Italian Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don is on a grand tour around Italy, this time visiting gardens both in Florence and around the sun-scorched Tuscan countryside. Monty discovers how, like a piece of sculpture, renaissance gardens were created as works of art, and how a group of Edwardian ex-pats mistakenly reinforced the idea that formal Italian gardens were flowerless.

programme.title: Monty Don's Italian Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don continues his grand garden tour of Italy. This time he is in Naples to visit some of the most sublime, romantic retreats in southern Italy. After all the formality of the green gardens around Rome and Florence, Monty seeks out idyllic horticultural gems set against the dramatic scenery of the Amalfi Coast to discover why, in the south, gardens are informal and, above all, filled with flowers.

programme.title: Monty Don's Italian Gardens
programme.sub-title: The Veneto, Lucca and The Lakes
programme.desc: Monty Don concludes his garden tour of Italy in the wealthy north where, in the Veneto and Lucca, extravagant pleasure gardens were created for flirtation and fun. In the Lakes, Monty meets up with the garden society, the Grandi Giardini Italiani, to peer over the fence at luxurious creations of the rich and famous. The journey ends on Lake Maggiore to see the most theatrical horticultural gem of Italy.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich Brothers meet Arvinder and Yolanda, a couple juggling older parents, twin babies and busy careers. Their garden has fallen into disrepair, so the team heads to south London to make a space where the family can stop and reflect.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Leicester the team meet Paul, a personal trainer who would like to use his space for clients, as well as for relaxing with friends and family. Can the team give him an all-purpose garden that also takes into consideration his disability?

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers are tasked with bringing a tropical vibe to a sparse garden in Lincolnshire. Lizzie and Mike want the colours and feel of an island getaway, so which design will they choose?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty has plans for interest and colour in his garden next year, from adding a rose to the new Dog House to planting daffodil bulbs and sowing sweet pea seeds.

programme.title: Gardeners' World

programme.desc: When Stephani Parze went missing, her family found themselves caught in a recurring nightmare. For 87 days, they and police searched for Stephanie. Her body was eventually found and prosecutors concluded she was killed by an obsessed ex-boyfriend....

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.sub-title: Armed and Obsessed: Lennon's Final Hours
programme.desc: Revealing details about one of the saddest crimes in music history, the murder of John Lennon. After years of investigating, we weave the final hours of the music legend's life. It was 1980 and Lennon is recording new music for the first time in y...

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.sub-title: Joel Rifkin: An American Serial Killer
programme.desc: On June 28, 1993, NY State Troopers tried to pull over a landscaper who was driving around with a missing license plate. That routine traffic stop ended with a high-speed chase, a crash, and the discovery of a dead body inside Joel Rifkin's truck.

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: Nelson Counne is accused of defrauding at least a dozen women of over $1.25 million. He promised to pay them back with ten times the amount, but never did. N12 Crime Files confronts him, calling him "The Worst Boyfriend on the Upper Eastside".

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: For years, a powerful New Jersey sheriff sexually abused kids in custody. County officials were warned but did nothing to stop it. News12 unravels three decades of secrets and follows the victims on their quest for justice.

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: In 2016, Sara Stern's car was found on a bridge in Belmar, NJ, leading to speculation she had committed suicide. It was later revealed her childhood friend, Sean McAtasney, had murdered her for her inheritance money. He later confessed to the crime..

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: In a drugstore robbery gone wrong, prescription drug addict David Laffer commits one of the worst mass shootings in Long Island's history as he kills a pharmacist, a 71-year-old clerk, and two shoppers.

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: Thirty-four years after the murder of Joan Wertkin went cold, with new information released to News12 CT, police say there are new leads and evidence. Wertkin was a mother of two from Westport, CT whose body was found strangled and still on fire t...

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: Richard Cottingham, known as the "Torso Killer," is connected to five killings on Long Island. The married father of three worked in New York City and claimed to kill over a100 women, many throughout the tri state area.Families of those killed wen...

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: Lisa Marie Velasquez's dismembered body was found in trash bags at two different parks in the Bronx after she was killed by her friend's abusive boyfriend with a hammer. The murder was eerily similar to the killing of her own mother, which Lisa Ma...

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Real or imaginary? It doesn't matter which as Bob Ross captures a great mountain and forest scene on specially cut plywood!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross shows you how two beautiful mountain and evergreen oval scenes can be harmonized into one memorable masterpiece.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Guest artist John Thamm easily creates a realistic face and head form on a shellacked surface with just a few intriguing strokes.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a breathtaking, misty wooded scene with tiny trickling creek that's fast and easy to recreate.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Visit a quaint little home with Bob Ross and experience the beauty of tender morn in the stillness of the forest; what a delightful painting.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Lovely soft colors flow from the magnificent brushes of Bob Ross in this lovely warm winter painting … don't miss seeing this pretty scene unfold.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Pick up your brushes and paint along with Bob Ross - he creates a beautiful, rocky stream gently rippling through the forest mist in an oval.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a spectacular mountain scene in a faux matte; a real masterpiece cleverly framed in an intriguing new idea!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Under the most glorious of skies, enjoy the rhythmic flow of deep sea waters crashing and splashing upon the surrounding rocks and cliffs - Bob Ross style!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: A painting to reflect the essence of fall, bursting forth in a profusion of brilliant colors as Bob Ross creates a haven of tranquility on canvas.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Join Bob Ross today as he paints a huge, single stately oak, just on the edge of the forest, its spreading branches and foliage providing comfort and shelter.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a spectacular warm winter painting where snow-covered mountains, trees and bushes lend an alluring sense of beauty in an oval.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Travel down an abandoned road with Bob Ross and stumble upon an old weathered barn that has seen better days - what dreams are made of.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Colors transforming, cool breezes and rushing waters - Bob Ross lets his paint brushes do all the talking in the magnificent mountain scene.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Happen upon this sweet cabin in the woods; Bob Ross paints a time-forgotten treasure that comes to life in a soft, warm landscape scene.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob paints a thawing landscape.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a beautiful golden warm waterfall scene, employing a lovely almost watercolor-looking effect. Come join in the fun!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: With guest artist Steve Ross.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: In an almost watercolor appearance, Bob Ross paints this amazing secluded waterfall masterpiece with interesting tree and rock formations.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob paints a path in the woods.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Hidden far from the highways of the South are lovely wetlands teaming with moss-clad trees and birds; Bob Ross shares a rare glimpse of beauty!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: From a quiet spot by this smooth-stoned river, join Bob Ross for a very, very distant view of a lovely snow-covered mountain.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a moment in time as the sea finds itself in one of nature's sudden cloudbursts; experience another oval masterpiece!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: In the golden glow of sunrise, join Bob Ross for a refreshing look at early morning where anglers work and thrive - all inside an uniquely framed painting.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Dry mountains, spiny shrubs and cacti, take a drive deep into the desert with Bob Ross and enjoy this fantastic oval surprise!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Talented guest painter Steve Ross shows you how to create a mirror-like lake spanning the width of snowy peaks and foothills.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Layers and layers of mountains, as far as the eye can see -- Bob Ross shows us the secret to creating a larger-than-life landscape.

programme.title: Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob uses monochromatic tones to convey the icy coldness of a silvery lake surrounded by lacy trees and bushes.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: An abandoned shipping boat hugs a pier in a water scene that Bob Ross completes with some charming old dockside storage sheds.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Watch Bob Ross create a sweet little waterfall in the middle of a protective forest of glorious early autumn foliage.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Swim in the warm water, nap in the luxurious green grass - today Bob Ross creates a wonderful summer mountain scene.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: An approaching storm and dramatic sky set the mood for this unique seascape. Bob Ross uses a canvas painted half black and half white.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Take a journey with Bob Ross and discover the beauty of a shimmering pool gently cradled by a soft, beautiful and bright forest.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Take a relaxing walk with Bob Ross through a bright green grassy forest, where a smooth crystal waterway flows so, so quietly.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross' exciting use of blue, yellow and orange hues brings vibrancy to this wood side setting and dream cabin beside a clear blue lake.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross' gentle brush strokes create bending palm trees on the edge of a sea coast, just before the water reaches its high point.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross' gentle brush strokes create bending palm trees on the edge of a sea coast, just before the water reaches its high point.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures a real 'dreamscape' on canvas -- soft rolling hills, birch trees and pretty lake; paint along and add your own touches.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: One of nature's perfect creations -- the misty, splashing waterfall -- is painted by Bob Ross, in all its magnificent splendor.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: See the amazing glacier-like peaks Bob Ross creates in this icy panorama; grab your painting knife and do this one too.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Watch and learn as Bob Ross unfolds this beautifully soft wooded scene with gently rippling brook, all inside an oval shape.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates an oval scene with warm brown and gold tones that captures the essence of a lonely forest river.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Travel with Bob Ross along the country back roads as he paints a quiet little home on a meadow with a beautiful, serene sky.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Surround yourself with this gorgeous Bob Ross painting; there's plenty of thick, velvety green grass, trees and foliage to just lose yourself in!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Such magnificent power and vibrant color in the sea and sky! Watch Bob Ross as he tries to tame this beautiful ocean painting.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross presents a really unique idea using black gesso -- two lovely paintings in one -- summer and winter scenes overlapping on the same canvas!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: This beautiful lake in the glen will leave you fully rested and at peace with the world; Bob Ross creates a quiet mountain masterpiece.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Join Bob Ross on a warm winter morning, the surroundings still undisturbed by Nature's little creatures; enjoy the freshness of a new day not yet awake.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Travel with Bob Ross to a secluded waterfall deep in the mountains, standing tall and proud. This magnificent scene instills comfort and joy.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Breaking the silence of a misty morning is a rippling brook through the forest. Join Bob Ross as he takes you to this favorite escape.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Take a leisurely hike down a weathered pathway with Bob Ross and happen upon an old cabin; create a beautiful oval painting you can be proud of.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Experience the blissful pleasure of the coldest season under a splendid pastel sky with Bob Ross; enjoy this fabulous mountain masterpiece.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Paint along with Bob Ross and discover the beauty of a secluded log home with only quiet pond and rustic trees for companionship.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Come visit the coast with Bob Ross and paint a glorious ocean-scape; enjoy the solitude and gentle rhythm of the waves caressing the shoreline.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting With Bob Ross
programme.desc: Paint along with Bob Ross and discover what autumn beauty unfolds as those exotic orange and gold colors appear on canvas.

programme.desc: NCIS works to solve the murder of a senator's daughter who has a rebellious reputation and large online following; Jimmy gets competitive with an old college rival.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: NCIS must investigate a string of Russian spy encounters that may be linked to a suspicious murder; Jimmy accidentally makes a confession that could change the course of his relationship with Knight.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Agent Torres finds himself in prison as the team tries to stop an impending terror attack on U.S. soil.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team must help Torres when he puts his future at stake by confronting the man who tormented his family when he was a child.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: An African teenager, whose father is an alleged war criminal, is abducted.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: Toby's telepathy acts as surveillance when Michelle goes under cover to expose an art-theft ring.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: Toby and Michelle investigate the connection between a cult and three missing young women.

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.sub-title: Holapu Ke Ahi, Koe Iho Ka Lehu
programme.desc: Adam's mission to take down organized crime goes sideways when the bait he uses falls into the wrong hands; Adam begins to doubt Jessie's allegiance.

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: The ex-wife of the man who shot Danny reveals how Danny helped save her life; Tani and Junior are assigned to walk the beat as uniformed officers for the day; Adam is framed for the murder of the crime boss he has been tracking.

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.sub-title: Aohe Mea Make I Ka Hewa; Make No I Ka Mihi Ole
programme.desc: A hit man McGarrett's father failed to arrest comes to McGarrett to finally confess his crimes and admit where he buried his victims; Jessie makes a dangerous decision that could ruin Adam's life.

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.sub-title: He Lokomaika'i Ka Manu O Kaiona
programme.desc: Catherine Rollins recruits McGarrett and Jerry to help track down uranium thought to be hidden on an abandoned Hawaiian island before a terrorist makes dirty bombs with it; Junior gets trapped in a ravine.

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: Harry Langford enlists McGarrett and Danny's help to track down a teenage British royal who has run away while under his protection; Grover, Tani and Junior work a 25-year-old murder case after a mysterious videotape arrives at headquarters.

programme.title: Blue Bloods
programme.desc: Frank is taken by surprise during an on-air interview; Danny and his new boss clash as they investigate a drive-by shooing.

programme.title: Blue Bloods
programme.desc: When an officer's body camera malfunctions during an altercation, Frank is faced with public backlash; Baez and Danny step in when a witness is denied protection by the U.S. Marshals Service; Erin looks into a rape claim made by a friend's daughter.

programme.title: Blue Bloods
programme.desc: When three prominent community leaders are murdered, they seem to be casualties of hate crimes; after being mugged, Linda decides to carry a gun.

programme.title: Blue Bloods
programme.desc: Frank looks for evidence against a suspected cop killer; Danny and Baez investigate the murders of stars of adult films.

programme.title: Blue Bloods
programme.desc: When a group of highly trained Army veterans robs a bank, Danny feels conflicted; a street artist is responsible for a bomb scare in a subway.

programme.title: Blue Bloods
programme.desc: A homeless teenager claims his aunt was murdered by her boyfriend; Erin is replaced on a case involving a corrections officer.

programme.title: Blue Bloods
programme.desc: When a homeowner shoots an intruder, surprising information is revealed; Erin starts to doubt herself when a 12-year-old case is reopened.

programme.title: Blue Bloods
programme.desc: A resident of the low-income section of a luxury apartment building is murdered; Frank is upset with a detective who takes advantage of legal loopholes.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.
programme.desc: After arriving in Chicago to assist Voight and the team with a rape/murder case that has distinct similarities to a decade-old case from New York, Sgt. Benson asks Fin and Amaro to help; the prime suspect asks for a meeting with Lindsay.

programme.title: Born Yesterday
programme.desc: Brassy blonde moll Billie Dawn (Judy Holliday) hits Washington, D.C., with her unscrupulous millionaire sugar daddy, Harry Brock (Broderick Crawford), and his sleazy lawyer, Jim Devery (Howard St. John), who has been pressuring Harry to marry Billie by pointing out that a wife cannot be forced to testify against her husband. In an effort to make Billie more socially acceptable, Harry hires journalist Paul Verrall (William Holden) to smarten her up -- and sparks soon fly between the pair.

programme.title: To Sir, With Love
programme.desc: American Mark Thackeray (Sidney Poitier) recently received his degree in engineering, but cannot find work. To make ends meet, he takes a job as a teacher in a rough London East End school populated mostly with troublemakers who were rejected from other schools for their behavior. While the students at first see Thackeray as just another teacher open for ridicule and bullying, his calm demeanor and desire to see them succeed gradually earn him their respect.

programme.title: Easy Rider
programme.desc: Two hippie bikers embark on a journey to discover "the real America" and take the ultimate bad trip in this landmark American film.

programme.title: Anatomy of a Murder
programme.desc: Semi-retired Michigan lawyer Paul Biegler (James Stewart) takes the case of Army Lt. Manion (Ben Gazzara), who murdered a local innkeeper after his wife (Lee Remick) claimed that he raped her. Over the course of an extensive trial, Biegler parries with District Attorney Lodwick (Brooks West) and out-of-town prosecutor Claude Dancer (George C. Scott) to set his client free, but his case rests on the victim's mysterious business partner (Kathryn Grant), who's hiding a dark secret.

programme.title: In Cold Blood
programme.desc: Based on the book by Truman Capote.

programme.title: Doctor Faustus
programme.desc: Frustrated with the limits of his knowledge, Dr. Faustus calls upon Lucifer and offers his soul in return for 24 years of hedonism and invincibility.

programme.title: Decameron Nights
programme.desc: While trying to woo a noblewoman in Florence, Italian poet Boccaccio tells three tales of love in this romance based on the author's writings.

programme.title: Born Free
programme.desc: A British couple in Kenya that raises an orphaned lion cub to adulthood train her to live in the wild when a warden demands she be sent to a zoo.

programme.title: Don't Knock the Twist
programme.desc: Dancers prepare for a TV special on the twist in this musical featuring performances of chart-topping hits from the heyday of rock 'n' roll.

programme.title: The Lone Wolf Takes a Chance
programme.desc: A former jewel thief (Warren William) is accused of murder after betting the police inspector that he can stay out of trouble for 24 hours.

programme.title: Adam Had Four Sons
programme.desc: A French governess becomes the center of a wealthy New York family with four boys after it suffers the losses of its matriarch, business and estate.

programme.title: Rockin' in the Rockies
programme.desc: Shorty Williams (Moe Howard) is put in charge of his cousin's ranch, but he is more concerned with becoming a prospector. He comes across two drifters (Larry Fine, Curly Howard) who just got lucky at the roulette table and befriends them as possible investors. Shorty also meets singer Betty (Gladys Blake) and her partner (Mary Beth Hughes) and convinces them to stay on the ranch along with the vagrants. Unfortunately, Shorty's cousin, Rusty (Jay Kirby), returns early and isn't pleased.

programme.title: Baby the Rain Must Fall
programme.desc: After his release from prison, an aspiring country singer tries to go straight, but his dream of a singing career puts this plan in danger.

programme.title: Cover Girl
programme.desc: John Coudair (Otto Kruger) was wounded long ago when his fiancée called off their engagement, and when he spots the woman's granddaughter, comely stage performer Rusty Parker (Rita Hayworth), he's reminded of what he lost. A publishing mogul, he decides to put the naive young beauty on the cover of his magazine. Although he might lose her, Rusty's boyfriend, Danny McGuire (Gene Kelly), refuses to interfere with her path to success. Ultimately, she must decide between riches and romance.

programme.title: Drive a Crooked Road
programme.desc: Auto mechanic Mickey must give up his dream of becoming a professional race car driver after he is fooled into being a chump for some gangsters.

programme.title: Meet the Stewarts
programme.desc: When a boy from modest means marries his rich, pampered finacé, squabbles over budget threaten to tear the lovebirds apart in this charming rom-com.

programme.title: You Belong to Me
programme.desc: Rich playboy Peter Kirk (Henry Fonda) crashes into Dr. Helen Hunt (Barbara Stanwyck) on a ski slope and overplays his injuries so she will continue her medical attention. He declares his love, and, although Helen insists that her career will always come first, the two get married. Soon, Peter is consumed by jealous suspicions of his wife's male patients and embarrasses Helen several times. Fearful of driving her away, Peter launches into character-changing behavior to prove his love.

programme.title: The Duel at Silver Creek
programme.desc: A gang of outlaws is murdering miners and taking over their claims, but they cross the wrong man when they target the father of Luke Cromwell (Audie Murphy), a gunslinger known as the Silver Kid. The local marshal, Lightning Tyrone (Stephen McNally), is also tracking the gang, but receives a crippling injury during a gunfight. Tyrone deputizes Cromwell, and the two take on the murderers together, while trying to resist the charms of Opal (Faith Domergue), the new beauty in town.

programme.title: Gunpoint
programme.desc: Near the Wild West town of Lodgepole, Colo., Sheriff Chad Lucas (Audie Murphy) gets shot during a train robbery -- not by the robbers, but by his jealous deputy, Cap. Hold (Denver Pyle), who believes he should be sheriff. Left to die, Chad rallies and goes off in search of the train robbers. Tracking them down in New Mexico, Chad and saloon owner Nate (Warren Stevens) chase after gang leader Drago (Morgan Woodward), who has taken Chad's ex-lover Uvalde (Joan Staley) as a hostage.

programme.title: Gunsmoke
programme.desc: Hired gun Reb Kittridge (Audie Murphy) goes to work in Montana for Matt Telford (Donald Randolph), who is looking to take over Dan Saxon's (Paul Kelly) fertile cattle land. What Reb doesn't bargain for is becoming a landowner himself after winning Saxon's property in a card game. Suddenly, he must work with Saxon and daughter Rita (Susan Cabot) to run the cattle to a rail line in order to make a mortgage payment. To stop him, Telford hires Reb's former partner, Johnny Lake (Charles Drake).

programme.title: Posse From Hell
programme.desc: Based on the novel by Clair Huffaker.

programme.title: Hell Bent for Leather
programme.desc: An honest horse-trader (Audie Murphy) is framed for murder by a marshal (Stephen McNally) out to make a name for himself.

programme.title: Walk the Proud Land
programme.desc: Based on a true story, this film follows John Philip Clum (Audie Murphy), a government official sent to Arizona to broker peace between the Americans and the Apache people. Initially hesitant, the Americans are convinced by Clum to stop fighting, and they allow him to establish a police force on the reservation, trained by Clum's friend Tom Sweeney (Charles Drake). Meanwhile, a romantic rivalry begins between Clum's wife and an Apache woman (Anne Bancroft) who has taken refuge in Clum's home.

programme.title: Column South
programme.desc: A cavalry officer (Audie Murphy) defends the Navajo despite his captain (Robert Sterling) and exposes a general's Civil War plot.

programme.title: The Man Behind the Gun
programme.desc: An Army major (Randolph Scott) goes undercover to stop a plot to make Southern California a separate state.

programme.title: Riding Shotgun
programme.desc: Larry Delong (Randolph Scott) loses his sister and her son when they're gunned down by the notorious Dan Marady (James Millican) and his band of outlaws during a holdup. Delong takes a job as a stagecoach guard, hoping to run into the bandits and take revenge. Run into them he does, but numbers are on Marady's side, and they shoot up and rob Delong's coach. Delong barely escapes with his life. When he tries to alert the authorities, they implicate him in the crime, and he becomes a fugitive.

programme.title: The Cisco Kid
programme.desc: Cisco and Pancho are trapped by an ex-pirate who runs a slave camp, with Indian guards, in a secret valley with his gorgeous niece who wants no part of it.

programme.title: The Cisco Kid
programme.desc: Cisco and Pancho meet up with a woman who claims to be an insurance investigator tracking down a stolen diamond necklace.

programme.title: Walk the Proud Land
programme.desc: Based on a true story, this film follows John Philip Clum (Audie Murphy), a government official sent to Arizona to broker peace between the Americans and the Apache people. Initially hesitant, the Americans are convinced by Clum to stop fighting, and they allow him to establish a police force on the reservation, trained by Clum's friend Tom Sweeney (Charles Drake). Meanwhile, a romantic rivalry begins between Clum's wife and an Apache woman (Anne Bancroft) who has taken refuge in Clum's home.

programme.title: The Cimarron Kid
programme.desc: A paroled outlaw (Audie Murphy) tries to change for a rancher's daughter (Beverly Tyler), but the Dalton gang won't let him.

programme.title: Stories of the Century
programme.desc: The Younger Brothers believe their past with the Quantrill Raiders keeps them from living an honest life and start planning a daring bank robbery in Minnesota.

programme.title: Stories of the Century
programme.desc: John Wesley Hardin, the infamous killer, is herding cattle to Abilene when he shoots two Indians in cold blood. Detectives Matt & Frankie arrive in Kansas to bring him to justice.

programme.title: Stories of the Century
programme.desc: The 1850's California gold fields is home to notorious bandit Joaquin Murietta. Matt and Frankie arrive to track him down but he kidnaps Frankie, believing she owns his hacienda.

programme.title: Stories of the Century
programme.desc: Tiburcio Vasquez vows vengeance against Anglos after his sister Dolores is killed. Matt and Frankie try to stop his terror in Southern California after a railroad agent is killed.

programme.title: Tombstone Territory
programme.desc: Convicted of slugging and robbing a miner, Renee Carter is being taken to Yuma prison; when the miner dies, she must return for trial, but Hollister believes she is covering up for a gambler, Frank Fallon, who will try to prevent her return.

programme.title: Tombstone Territory
programme.desc: Sheriff Clay Hollister sends for a woman lawyer to settle a dispute between a mine owner and a local businessman; Clay is unaware that one of the men has been stealing gold and putting it in blocks of ice.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Lucas receives a series of mysterious threatening notes from an ex-convict with a grudge.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: After shooting a man in self-defense, Andy Moon turns to Lucas for protection from the man's vengeful brothers.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: To keep his invalid mother happy, an outlaw forces Lucas and Mark to go along with a masquerade that he is the owner of Lucas's ranch.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: A sharecropper enters his colt in a horse race hoping to win enough money to buy his farm from the owner who has bet on Mark McCain's entry.

programme.title: Yancy Derringer
programme.desc: Miss Duval's servant is killed trying to prevent her flower stand from being destroyed after a protection racket threatens to gain control of the French Market. In utter fear for their lives, the merchants refuse to step forward to Administrator Colton with an official accusation. Yancy and Pahoo decide to help the stall owners battle the thugs.

programme.title: Yancy Derringer
programme.sub-title: S01:E17 - The Night the Russians Landed
programme.desc: Yancy & Pahoo rescue Grand Duke Alexis, the hard-partying heir to the Russian throne, from a band of assassins. Colton agrees that the best chance to keep the Grand Duke safe is to rent Madame Francine's gambling club for the evening, but Francine's girls begin to wonder how safe they are from the Russian heir's roving eye!

programme.title: Bat Masterson

programme.title: Bat Masterson

programme.title: The Monuments Men
programme.desc: In a race against time, a crew of art historians and museum curators unite to recover renowned works of art stolen by Nazis before Hitler destroys them.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Ninja
programme.desc: Years after he was raised by a ninja clan in Japan, a clumsy but well-meaning oaf gets a chance to prove himself when a woman needs his protection.

programme.title: The Karate Kid
programme.desc: A troubled teenager finds guidance in an unorthodox karate master, who teaches him wisdom and self-control, helping him conquer his deepest fears.

programme.title: The Karate Kid Part II
programme.desc: After learning that his father is dying, karate master Mr. Miyagi (Noriyuki "Pat" Morita) returns home to Okinawa, bringing his protege, Daniel (Ralph Macchio), with him. In Japan, Miyagi is surprised to discover that his old sweetheart, Yukie (Nobu McCarthy), has remained single. Meanwhile, Daniel is attracted to Yukie's niece, Kumiko (Tamlyn Tomita). But romance must be put on hold while Daniel and Miyagi deal with local bullies and long-harbored grudges.

programme.title: The Karate Kid Part III
programme.desc: Cobra Kai karate instructor John Kreese (Martin Kove) is still brooding over the defeat handed to him by Daniel LaRusso (Ralph Macchio) and Mr. Miyagi (Noriyuki "Pat" Morita). With his slimy partner, Terry Silver (Thomas Ian Griffith), Kreese plans an intricate payback that involves hiring a mean-spirited ringer (Sean Kanan) to rob Daniel of his championship title. When Mr. Miyagi refuses to train Daniel, even under threat, the boy makes the mistake of turning to Terry for guidance.

programme.title: How Do You Know
programme.desc: An aging softball player finds herself caught between a playboy MLB pitcher and a white-collar executive on the hook for his father’s stock fraud.

programme.title: Peggy Sue Got Married
programme.desc: An unhappy woman named Peggy Sue tries to change her future when she inadvertently travels back in time to her senior year of high school.

programme.title: Dancer, Texas: Pop. 81
programme.desc: Four graduating high schoolers contemplate their future lives, deciding whether or not to leave their town of Dancer, Texas to move to Los Angeles.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: A young ad executive facing terminal cancer and expecting a child records his life's journey, confronting his past for his unborn child's future.

programme.title: Blonde Ambition
programme.desc: After lucking into a job in New York, a small-town girl must outwit saboteurs when she finds herself an unwitting pawn in a corporate takeover scheme.

programme.title: Jury Duty
programme.desc: A jobless freeloader gets assigned to jury duty for a murder trial and stops at nothing to keep the deliberations going as long as possible.

programme.title: Stealing Harvard
programme.desc: A home health-care exec under the thumb of his fiancée’s father is forced into petty crime to finance his niece's first year in the Ivy Leagues.

programme.title: Quicksilver
programme.desc: Losing his shirt after a bad day on the market, a stock trader makes ends meet as a bicycle courier and uncovers sinister web of intrigue and murder.

programme.title: Facing The Giants
programme.desc: In this inspiring tale, a Christian high school football coach uses his undying faith to battle the giants of fear and failure.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Think lobsters are pricey? They’ve got nothing on “glass eels,” now a beyond-booming industry on the coast of Maine. We visit Ellsworth, where at today’s price of $2,200 a pound, there’s heavy competition to net the tiny, wriggling baby eels for export to Asia. We meet some of the lucky few licensed in the glass eel trade, including the so-called “godfather” of the industry, as well as ordinary Mainers whose fortunes have changed thanks to a coveted license. And we talk to Maine law enforcement about the early days of the eel trade, a wild-west scene replete with guns, cash – and eels.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Those topics that just might be off the table in your house, or the ones that make you turn just a little bit red. Tonight, Chronicle is pulling back the curtain on some “taboo topics” - from politics at the dinner table to trips to the restroom.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From nostalgic balsa wood airplanes, to sophisticated space suits made in Worcester – including the suit Sunita Williams wore on her most recent and much-longer-than-expected visit to space. Tonight Nicole Estaphan brings us all things “sky-high”.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: New Englanders love spending time by the water, even in winter. Anthony Everett visits Chatham, on Cape Cod, where he meets a business exec turned fine art photographer, chronicles the rebirth of a local movie theater, and grabs a bite at…the airport? Shayna Seymour visits coastal Freeport, Maine – during a snowstorm!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: They call it the “Bidd-aissance” – the rebirth underway in Biddeford, Maine. Tonight Anthony Everett heads to the former mill town, now becoming a foodie mecca. Finally, Shayna Seymour is exploring Westerly, R.I., where she gets spooked on a ghost tour, shops for books, and overnights at the historic Weekapaug Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Shayna Seymour heads to Lake Sunapee in New Hampshire, where the ice is out, a local farm is bursting with life, and the community is gearing up for peak tourist season.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan heads north to explore lake life in Naples, Maine, where she hops aboard a paddlewheeler on Long Lake; stumbles across Shaquille O’Neal’s sneaker at a unique shop; and bites into a “Sebagel” at a local bagel shop

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: They call it the “Bidd-aissance” – the rebirth underway in Biddeford, Maine. Tonight Anthony Everett heads to the former mill town, now fast becoming a foodie mecca. He samples local seafood; visits historic City Theater; and checks out the city’s hip new hotel. Erika Tarantal heads south to Marion, Mass., stopping in its quaint downtown; exploring the local natural history museum and a historic public golf course; and sampling the local catch. Finally, Shayna Seymour is exploring Westerly, R.I., where she gets spooked on a ghost tour, shops for books, and overnights at the historic Weekapaug Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett heads to midcoast Maine, where in Rockport he discovers a tiny bookshop with an inspiring origin story; encounters some special cows at a working farm/nature preserve; and takes in the ocean views from the classic Samoset Resort.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett uses a tank and a ferry to get to this summer destination – Block Island. He gets a taste of summer life on the Block, hits up local restaurant The Oar and the revamped Block Island Beach House, and meets the gifted artist behind the charming “glass float” project. Meanwhile, Shayna Seymour pays a summer visit to ever-charming Mystic, Conn. She frolics with the sea life at Mystic Aquarium, travels back in time at Mystic Seaport Museum, makes a quick stop at Mystic Pizza (naturally) and checks out the charming Whalers Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Erika Tarantal hits Providence, R.I., where she checks out the art scene, tours a historic hotel, and even takes a blacksmithing class. While Shayna Seymour visits Portsmouth, N.H and Nicole Estaphan goes to Portland, Maine.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re headed north, south, and east for these summer escapes.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we visit coastal communities that are waking up from their winter slumber. Shayna Seymour heads north to Kennebunkport, Maine, where she samples ice cream with outrageous flavors at Rococo Dessert Bar, visits an artisan boutique, and beds down at the historic Kennebunkport Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If winter has you chilled to the bone, we have ideas to warm you up! Erika Tarantal is “skinning” up Black Mountain in Jackson, N.H., then warming up at the Villa Hygge Hotel in North Conway, where she introduces us to the cozy Scandinavian concept of “hygge.” Shayna Seymour heads to Vermont’s Mad River Valley, where diners at the original American Flatbread can dine fireside in their own private cabins. And Nicole Estaphan dines in an igloo in Portland, Maine, and visits a hotel in nearby Cape Elizabeth, where the stories of dog rescues will warm the heart.

programme.title: Wellness Hour: Restoring Your Smile
programme.desc: The Benefits Of Dental Implants.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Shayna Seymour is heading up to York, Maine to experience a slice of this summertime haven at the height of fall. From a cliff-side resort to a brewery steeped in family tradition - Shayna discovers why fall is perfect along the Maine coast.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Forget the jingle bells and candy canes – New Englanders have some unusual holiday habits!

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: SportsCenter 5 OT
programme.desc: The latest scores, updates, and analysis from the WCVB Sports Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s not just “Dry January.” Polling indicates more Americans are tempering their alcohol intake, and tonight we talk to consumers, businesses, and experts about the changes.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Jamaica Plain is constantly evolving but one constant is Centre Street. We make the rounds of Boston’s official “Latin Quarter” and talk with residents, business owners, and activists about what’s changed and what hasn’t - namely, its deep sense of community.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein is back with all new stories of New Englanders following their passions.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re visiting “Mill” towns tonight -- that’s, towns that begin with M-I-L-L (sorry, Milton and Milford!). There are only three “Mill-,” towns in Massachusetts and Ted Reinstein is exploring them all. In Millis, Ted discovers a wine shop where one can find the perfect bottle to pair with anything from fine dining to “ketchup-smothered food.” Then, he is off to Millbury for a tour of the historic Asa Waters Mansion. He also meets a local cake decorator whose designs may be “too hot for TV.” Finally, in Millville, Ted checks in with the Millville High School band marching toward another state title.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Ted Reinstein explores a snowy stretch of highway between Vermont’s largest city (Burlington) and its capital city (Montpelier). In Richmond he visits a community ski hill that has bred Olympians, and the town’s rather unusual round landmark.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From Saratoga Springs to West Port, find out where adventures await just a ‘Tank Away.’

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Bringing Down The House: The Bombing of Harvey's Casino
programme.desc: In the summer of 1980, two men wheeled what looked like an IBM computer into a Lake Tahoe casino. The events that followed rocked the industry, baffled bomb technicians, and changed the lives of a criminal’s and a casino magnate’s family forever.

programme.title: The Lost Children of Carlisle
programme.desc: A powerful look at the history of Native American boarding schools as told through the stories of tribal members and their surviving family members, who endured an educational system founded on the concept “Kill the Indian in him, and save the man.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett heads to the heart of New Hampshire’s Monadnock region to visit the often-overlooked town of Hancock, where he meets the artist behind curious woodland creatures, and visits the longest continuously-operating inn in New Hampshire. Nicole Estaphan keeps the adventure rolling in Quechee, Vermont with a Segway tour guide who doubles as the local Toy Museum curator and collector. Meanwhile, Shayna Seymour visits Rhode Island’s largest greenhouse at Clark Farms, dines in a “hobbit house,” and while in South County visits with new café owners Ashley and Jared, aka the “most dramatic” couple from ABC’s “Bachelor in Paradise,” who are now in the restaurant biz.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.sub-title: Chronicle: A Tank Away Rare BnB
programme.desc: Iconic, aluminum travel trailers sound like a throwback, and they are. But at an Airstream “campground” on Cape Cod, Erika Tarantal discovers this retro vehicle has undergone some modern upgrades. Nicole Estaphan spends the night in a refurbished 100-year-old train caboose in Chester, Mass. And once you crash at one vehicle-turned-hotel, why not try another? Nicole spends the night on a 43-foot motor yacht moored in Charlestown. Join us as we check-in to some “Rare” BnBs.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we take a trip to Eliot, Maine, where we check out Sandy Hill Farm, an outdoor light show. A couple of hours to the west and north we explore Hanover, New Hampshire a charming community that is a hub of culture, dining, and outdoor activity.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.sub-title: Chronicle: A Walk in the Park
programme.desc: You’ve heard his name… but maybe not realized his impact. Frederick Law Olmsted’s landscape designs include New York’s Central Park, the U.S. Capitol grounds, and of course, Boston’s Emerald Necklace. In honor of Olmsted’s 200th birthday we’re celebrating his achievements – visiting his home studio in Brookline and touring a private, Olmsted-designed estate that has been perfectly preserved. We also learn about Olmsted’s belief that parks are essential to democracy, and should always be free and open to all.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Exploring Boston’s Historic 7-mile street, ‘Adams Street.’

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: WCVB

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In January 2022 the City of Boston dismantled a tent encampment in a neighborhood plagued by illicit drug use and violence. So…what happened after that? We meet people who have resettled in transitional housing, get a look inside the cottages where some now receive services, and ride along with city outreach workers trying to serve those who are still struggling at Mass and Cass. We also talk to business owners and city officials trying to avoid a return to the dangerous status quo and devise a long-term solution to the crisis.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In July, catastrophic floods brought parts of Vermont to its knees. Farms in western Massachusetts suffered, too. Beginning immediately after the floods, Chronicle began talking with Vermonters whose homes, businesses, and communities were devastated by this shock from Mother Nature. From Barre, Montpelier, and Ludlow, Vt., we bring you their stories – and their optimism about the future (leaf peepers welcome!). We also talk to Massachusetts farmers whose crops were wiped out, to find out how they’re rebuilding.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Dinner in a treehouse, or under a tree? Tonight we try both, as we head out in search of unique spots to enjoy a meal al fresco.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Explore the Main streets and backroads of Amherst, MA.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As he does every year, Anthony Everett spent time on the water in 2021. This year, that time also included joining Coast Guard officers training for search and rescue missions. He also drove a military tank and reported on the growing division and spread of misinformation in our country. Tonight, Anthony looks back at his favorite stories of 2021.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Christopher is barely 21, already has history of domestic abuse and assault, and now he's facing major time in a room without a view. This hunt won't be easy for Dog.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog lectures high school students about the dangers of drugs before going after Joseph, a dangerous fugitive who won't be easy to capture. Dog gets special help from a UPS driver,

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Justin is the low man on the totem pole in the Chapman clan, and this means that the rest of the team is always teasing him.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and Beth have to be at the state capitol bright and early to receive an award for their fight against ice in Hawaii. Afterwards, they're inspired to start the hunt for Shanna, an alleged ice user and identity thief with $80,000 in bonds.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Things are seldom what they seem in the world of bounty hunting and it is through this maze of illusions that Dog pursues his prey.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: It's Beth's birthday and Dog knows just what she needs, whether she knows it or not! He surprises Beth with Delilah, a Papillion puppy small enough to sit in the palm of her hand.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: What do you do when you have a fugitive you not only want to find, but believe can be helped back onto the straight and narrow? If you're Mary Ellen you call you good friend Dog.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: This fugitive is hard to pin down in Honolulu's downtown district--moving from hostel to hotel to apartment high-rise. But as the hunt evolves, Dog discovers that this alleged burglar isn't the only family member wanted by the law.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog decides to help two of his bondsmen buddies, Dave "Red" Widhelm and "Downtown" Bobby Brown. First up is Red, who has a client named Alvin who failed to appear on a $20,000 bond with charges of drug possession and distribution pending.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is trying to cut down on smoking, but it's not easy with his high-stress job. Today, he must track down Walter, a fugitive who believes that the Canadian military has planted a chip in his tooth.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog tries to nab a fugitive named Wayne by tricking him. Dog calls Wayne to tell him that he's won a new vacuum cleaner and he should stick around to get it.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Hunting down a fugitive known as "Bozo," Dog gets help from an unexpected source - Bozo's father-in-law. When they find Bozo, he protests his wife-beating charge, saying he never hit her with his fist, only his open hand.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and his team chase down a bail jumper and he tries to help the criminal turn his life around with words of wisdom.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the team begin their day by judging the local elementary school's talent show, where they declare all the kids to be winners.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Going after two fugitives in one day is tough enough without the facing Colorado's notoriously unpredictable weather. Today it's the threat of sever thunderstorm, hail and area wide tornado warnings.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today Leland and Dog make a quick visit to the police supply store to get some new gadgets for the team. But they can't linger because they've also got a fugitive to catch. Their target is reputed to have an explosive temper.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's relentless ruthlessness is being put to the test on the search for the elusive fugitive, Marco. Dog is tracking him across Colorado, from Grand Junction to Denver.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the posse travel to the Big Island to help Leland track down a small-time Bonnie and Clyde couple. Their crime: theft, including allegedly stealing a pair of Buddha statues for resale. But this only masks deeper problems with the couple.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is in Denver helping Bobby Brown to track down a fugitive with a long rap sheet. But unfortunately they have no picture of the felon to work with, so the challenges are big on this one.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the team really have their hands full when they go after a tough guy named Nunu, who leads them on a chase through apartment complexes and a shopping mall before he's finally captured. But Dog is charmed by Nunu's unquenchable

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog answers two very different calls for help from women. In the first case, he helps Leland protect Maria, who's being threatened by her violent husband.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog thinks he's got an average day of bounty hunting ahead of him, but he couldn't be more wrong. Beth calls a client, Daphne, whose bond they have to revoke and makes a shocking discovery.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter

programme.desc: Ancient astronaut theorist Giorgio A. Tsoukalos explores the latest scientific discoveries at some of the world's most ancient places to find evidence that extraterrestrials visited Egypt in the distant past.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Examining various events that are said to have been directed by the hand of the Almighty, such as the Great Flood, the Plagues of Egypt, Sodom and Gomorrah, and Moses parting the Red Sea.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Deadly dances. Masters of manipulation. Mass suicides. Murder. Many of the world's deadliest cults ventured down dark, dangerous rabbit-holes of twisted ideologies.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Every year, thousands of people report looking up at the sky and witnessing unidentified flying objects. A vast majority of these strange and disturbing incidents take place in the same locations on Earth. Is it possible that extraterrestrial spacecraft are repeatedly visiting Earth at so-called "UFO Hot Spots?"

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Sacred waters with remarkable healing powers; ancient mystics who lived for hundreds of years; a scientific breakthrough may provide the answers to eternal life.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: As a new era of exploration of the Red Planet begins, the Red Planet's mysteries might be close to getting solved; ancient cataclysms are believed to have left Mars barren, but science continues to find clues that this red planet was once blue.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: From the ancient Babylonians to modern-day urban explorers, mankind has always been fascinated by abandoned places; discovering the allure that draws people to post-apocalyptic landscapes.

programme.desc: Sunken ships filled with priceless cargo; lost cities of gold; ancient temples concealing untold riches; there's something about these hidden treasures that captures the human imagination.
